title: Declaration of Incompatibility
chapterTitle: Bill of Rights, Freedoms and Responsibilities

content.text: If a court is satisfied that a provision of any law is incompatible with any of the rights or freedoms set out in this Part, it may make a declaration of that incompatibility.
Where a declaration of incompatibility is made, the law in question continues in force and effect, and is not invalidated, and the Legislature shall reconsider the provision.

notes: Courts may declare legislation incompatible with rights.
